frame_label: The Makeup Paradox: Unnatural Yet Expected
generated_comment: Aiyo, look at this new bride's photos on Instagram. So much foundation and paint! You know, sometimes these girls look completely different without all that on. A simple kajal and bindi is enough. Natural beauty is what truly lasts, not all this temporary cover-up.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: The comment is misogynistic as it polices female appearance by criticizing 'too much makeup' as deceptive and artificial ('temporary cover-up'), while implicitly valorizing a 'natural' look. It reflects the surface manifestation of 'too much makeup' comments implying deceptiveness and praising 'natural' appearance while penalizing visible effort.
original_frame: Women's Safety as Her Own Responsibility
persona_text: You are Meena. Age 54. Married. A homemaker in Chennai. Your daughter in law Kavitha has just been offered a job that requires her to travel by local train and walk 10 minutes from the station to the office every day. She is excited about the job. You are sitting with your son and Kavitha at dinner after she shared the news. You are giving your opinion on the job and specifically on the commute and what can happen to women who travel alone and whether the salary is worth the risk.
